title: States Sue President Trump Over New Tariffs
summary: Some states are suing President Donald Trump because they don't like his new tariffs. Tariffs are taxes on things brought into the country from other places. Trump says these taxes help America by reducing trade deficits, which means we buy more from other countries than they buy from us. But the states, led by Oregon, Arizona, California, and New York, think Trump is using too much power. They say the tariffs will make things more expensive for families and businesses. The lawsuit happened after Trump lost a case at the Supreme Court and used a different law to put the tariffs in place. The White House says Trump is allowed to do this to help the country. This is a big legal fight about who can make rules about trade.

background_read: This article is about a legal fight between some U.S. states and President Donald Trump over tariffs. Tariffs are taxes on goods from other countries, and they can affect prices for everyone. The states think Trump is using too much power by imposing these tariffs without proper reasons, while Trump believes they help America by balancing trade. This shows how government leaders can disagree on rules that impact money and trade in the country.
Article_Structure: WHO: President Donald Trump and states like Oregon, Arizona, California, and New York
WHAT: A lawsuit over new tariffs imposed by Trump
WHERE: In the United States, specifically Washington D.C. and federal courts
WHY: Because the states believe Trump is overstepping his power and the tariffs will raise costs

perspectives.description: The tariffs are illegal and will hurt families and businesses by increasing prices.
Trump is using his legal authority to help America by reducing trade deficits.
